body_text: In a pressure cooker or instant pot, cook whole Garam Masala in ghee till fragrant.
Toss in onions till clear, then add in rest of vegetables + ginger garlic paste. Cook till well combined and toasty.
Toss in rice and 3 cups of liquid, however much can of coconut milk is and the rest as water.
Add in relevant spices: salt, pepper, karam, biriyani masala, garam masala
Taste, liquid should be slightly salty and spicy.
Add in cilantro, start the pressure cooker and cook for 10 minutes.
Let pressure release slowly, then ready to serve.
Boil jumbo shells using box instructions and set aside to cool.
Preheat Oven to 400*F
In a saucepan, add olive oil and onion with a little salt, sauté till softened
Add in the meat, break into small pieces and cook till caramelized and brown. Then mix in chopped spinach. Let cool.
Into meat, mix: Ricotta, Mozzarella, seasonings, and the egg. Add in the caramelized meat and spinach and mix with the cheese.
In a large oven safe pan, layer half the marinara sauce on the bottom.
Fill shells with the cheese/meat filling and layer on top of the sauce.
Pour the remaining sauce over the shells and top with the remaining mozzarella.
Bake for 15/20 minutes, let cool for 5 before serving.
Stir together sauce till fully combined and sugar is dissolved.
Heat half the oil in a wok, once very hot, pour in beaten egg. Scramble till 90% done then remove from wok.
Add remaining oil to wok, saute the ginger garlic paste with some chilli flakes for a few seconds before pouring in the sauce and the water. Bring to a simmer.
Add in ramen block, let cook for 4 minutes, stirring.
Fold in scrambled eggs, peanuts and scallions.
In a pan, brown the butter, stirring constantly. As soon as it starts foaming, remove from heat and add in coffee powder to allow to bloom. Cool for at least 10 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk flour, corn starch, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
Into the browned butter, whisk in sugars and vanilla till glossy. Then add in the egg and the extra yolk, whisking till emulsified.
Then add in the dry ingredients and gently fold till barely combined. Then fold in chopped chocolate chunks till you don’t see anymore flour streaks.
Make 75g dough balls, shape into balls and place on a parchment lined baking sheet.
Let cookie dough chill covered in the in the fridge for 12 - 24 hours, this results in deeper caramelization.
Preheat oven to 340 °F. Line two trays with parchment. Place dough balls at least 2 inches apart.
Bake in preheated oven for 18–20 min, until edges are set but centers look soft and puffy.
Optionally shape with a round bowl and sprinkle flakey sea salt on top!
Let them cool on the tray for 10-15 minutes so they can set up, but then you can enjoy!
Wash all veggies, then chop: lettuce, radish, cucumber, bell pepper, tomatoes, herbs, and anything else.

In a large salad bowl, toss together all the chopped veggies and herbs. Throw in the drained chickpeas, then add salt, pepper, and za’atar. Add a generous drizzle of dressing (about ¼ cup) to taste and toss the salad thoroughly so everything is mixed really well.
Throw in crushed pita chips and mix at the end, or eat the chopped salad as a dip!
Defrost 2 filets of frozen code by placing them in room temperature water or thawing overnight.
Prep all aromatics: peel and slice garlic, slice an onion, some chilies and green onion (greens separated)
Pour 1 tbsp of oil into a pan along with all the aromatics and salt, sauté on medium-low till fragrant and the onion is translucent.
Pour in the coconut milk, add in the tbsp of bouillon then stir till fully combined.
Either toss in saffron tea at this point or a handful of chopped dill and fish sauce.

Now salt and pepper (and any other herb-y seasonings) at this point, turmeric, chilli flakes, taste till satisfied. Going overboard here is good. Once well seasoned, toss in the now thawed cod filets. Spoon some poaching liquid on top of the filets that might not be covered.
Bring liquid down to a simmer (very low), cover partially with a lid, then let cook for 10-25 minutes. Longer will just make it fall apart even more.
Serve on top of rice or remove the fish filets and toss orzo in to make an orzo soup!
Cook the noodles according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
Make sauce by combining all sauce ingredients.
To the pot used to cook the noodles, add in chicken broth and 1/2 of the sauce mixture. Bring to a simmer over medium heat, stir in the spinach. Keep warm.
To a skillet, add in the ground meat along with half the sauce. Cook till and brown all over, breaking it up as it cooks, for 5 minutes.
Deglaze with and a splash of Shaoxing Wine then add in minced mushrooms and shallot, and cook another 2-3 minutes. Slowly pour in the other half of the soy sauce mixture.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the sauce coats the meat and the meat begins to caramelize and get crispy, about 5 minutes before stirring in another 2-4 tablespoons chili oil.
Split the noodles into two bowls, pour over the warm broth, stir to combine. Spoon the meat over the noodles. Top with green onions and additional chili oil.
Wash strawberries by placing them in a bowl of one cup water, one cup white vinegar. Let sit for 10 minutes before rinsing thoroughly.
Dry each strawberry thoroughly before letting air dry completely.
Melt chocolate in a double boiler by placing each chocolate with some coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl and setting it over a small pot with an inch or two of simmering water.
Dip each strawberry in chocolate. Then place each strawberry on parchment paper to set.
Optional: Double dip into the chocolate for extra chocolate!
Let set on counter before lightly covering in plastic wrap and placing them in the fridge!
If using bamboo skewers, soak for 30 minutes
Prepare chicken marinade by mixing all the ingredients together.
Cover with a plastic wrap and let sit for atleast 20 minutes.
Optional: Skewer Chicken
Place chicken on a wire rack over a rimmed baking sheet under the broiler (about ~6 inches away), cook 2-3 minutes each side, flip once.
Preheat broiler to medium high heat.
Preheat the oven to 350 F and grease a loaf pan.
Beat sugar and butter in a bowl until smooth.
Beat in eggs, then bananas. Add flour and baking soda, stirring just until combined. Pour into the prepared pan.
Pour batter into the prepared loaf pan.
Bake in the preheated oven till a toothpick comes out clean (or till 205*), about 1 hour
Let cool for 10 minutes before removing it from the pan onto a cooling rack.
Let cool completely cutting into slices!
Mix ground pork with marinade, let sit for a few minutes while prepping everything else.
Chop onions finely, then put into a hot pan with oil and some ginger garlic paste.
Cook till translucent, then add pork. Spread flat then cook undisturbed on medium for 4 minutes. Once edges are crispy and caramelized, stir, breaking them up into very small chunks.
Once fully cooked, layer rice in bowls, top with spicy pork, place a soft-boiled egg, sprinkle scallions and optional garnishes. Drizzle extra chili oil or vinegar as desired.
In a large pot, sauté the sausage until browned and caramelized. Transfer sausage into another bowl.
In the same pot, add butter and sauté onions with some salt until translucent.
Add garlic and sauté for another minute until fragrant. (very briefly, don’t let it burn)
Add chicken broth, water, potatoes, salt, pepper,  chili flakes, parmesan rind and bring to a boil.
Boil until potatoes are tender. Stir in kale, and coconut milk.
Add sausage. Taste and add salt and pepper if needed. Serve garnished with grated parmesan cheese.
Marinate (velvet) the protein in soy sauce, baking soda, and white pepper.
Mix the sauce ingredients together. Then make a paste in the mortar with some garlic, chilies, and small sprinkle or kosher salt. (optionally add in some sichuan peppers for extra spice)
Chop all the veggies into slices or chunks.
Crack two eggs into a bowl, season and whisk thoroughly.
Then, into a hot pan, add half the oil, and spread protein evenly onto the pan, wait to get a good char before tossing. Once fully cooked, remove into a bowl.
Pour rest of the oil into the pan and once heated, toss in the eggs and scramble. Once 75% cooked, toss in onions (and bell pepper, carrots) along with the garlic chili paste.
Once the veggies are just a little tender, toss in the cooked rice and the sauce mixture.
Add salt, MSG, and red chili flakes here. Also toss in basil, cherry tomatoes, and spinach at this point.
Finish with some sesame oil and Sichuan oil and stir.
Top with lime, sliced jalapeno, and some sesame seeds.

recipe_name: Lime and Brown Sugar Glazed Salmon
body_text: Make a salt water brine and brine salmon for atleast 30 minutes. Then, fully pat salmon dry using a towel.

recipe_name: Lime and Brown Sugar Glazed Salmon
body_text: To a small saucepan set over medium heat, add sugar, honey, and butter. Cook, stirring continuously until sugar dissolves. Remove from the heat and whisk in the lime leaves, fish sauce, 1/2 tbsp vegetable oil, and ginger.

recipe_name: Lime and Brown Sugar Glazed Salmon
body_text: Set aside to cool.

recipe_name: Lime and Brown Sugar Glazed Salmon
body_text: To a large nonstick skillet set over medium-high heat, add remaining vegetable oil. Season the salmon with salt and pepper and place in the skillet, flesh side down. Cook until nicely seared, 2 to 3 minutes, then turn the fish over. Lower the heat to medium and brush the top of the salmon with brown sugar glaze. Continue to brush the salmon with the glaze and cook until no longer translucent in the center, about 6 minutes. Serve immediately.
Cut mushrooms up into small squares, and add into hot pan.
Add oil into pan and then shallot, garlic, and chili flakes.
Pour in orzo, stir, then splash of broth to deglaze the pan once it sticks.
Pour rest of the broth, mushrooms, and add in the freshly grated parmesan, and knob of butter
Let simmer for 10 minutes, add in more parmesan (if necessary), lemon juice, salt and pepper to taste.
Add spinach in and stir till wilted.
Mix in parsley and serve, top with truffle oil.

Combine all dough ingredients into a bowl, then mix till cohesive. Knead dough but note that it will be very sticky. If it’s too sticky, just use wet hands to handle the dough, avoiding adding too much extra flour.
Let rise on the counter for an hour or two, or in the fridge for up to 7 days.
On a 10 inch pan (either a well seasoned cast iron or non stick), pour a little bit of olive oil, some salt, chilli flakes, and oregano, then spread to coat the bottom of the pan.
Then place the dough in the center of the pan and spread with your fingers till it reaches the edge of the pan, push up the sides cause the pizza will shrink. Use wet hands for this process.
Rub the surface with a littleee more olive oil and let it rise again in the pan for 30 minutes. (The olive oil is important it keeps the pizza from drying out while it proofs)
Grate mozzarella and cut up any additional toppings.
Sauce:

Put the pizza on the stovetop on medium heat. Cook the dough till the bottom is a deep brown/black, you want it to be charred and to smell a little burnt (trust me). Shake and move the pizza around, lifting the edges to check its done-ness.

When the bottom is fully cooked, take the pan off the heat, then spread on the sauce (all the way to the edges) and top with cheese (again to the edges!!) Optionally add more toppings at this point and maybe even top with a little more cheese!
Now transfer the pan under the hot broiler, close the oven door and let bake till cheese is browned to your liking (~3 minutes)
Remove pizza from the oven, let cool just till it’s solid enough to move, then immediately transfer to a wire rack (sooner your transfer the pizza, the crispier it will be!)
Let cool before slicing and serve!
In a hot wok, add the mushrooms. Stir fry with no spices or oil till fragrant and toasty, then transfer into a separate bowl.
Gently wipe the wok to remove any residue, then add in the ghee till melted before adding in the whole spices. Once fragrant, add the onions.
Sauté until golden, then add ginger garlic paste. Fry till just a little toasted before deglazing with a splash of water.
Then stir in all the spices (except for kasthuri methi) along with the tomato paste and another 1/4 cup of water. Cook down till it thickens.
Once thickened, pour in coconut milk and mix well and bring to a rolling boil on high heat. Reduce the curry again, then add in the mushrooms.
Cook covered until the mushrooms are tender (adding water if the curry is too thick)
Add kasthuri methi along with salt to taste at this point.
Splash some lemon juice and garnish with coriander before serving.
Break down the chicken wings into flats and drumettes. Place them into a large bowl, then add salt and baking powder, tossing them together till they’re all evenly coated.
Transfer the wings onto a wire rack placed on top of a sheet tray (spaced apart so they don’t touch), and then refrigerate for a full 24 hours (4 hours minimum).
Then, turn on oven broiler to high w/ the rack about 8” away.

Now, we’ll broil the wings for 25 minutes total, flipping them every 5 minutes. So place the wings in, wait 5 minutes, take them out before flipping each one, then repeat till all are an even golden brown and crispy.
While cooking, making the sauces. Once all the wings are cooked, toss them in the sauce and serve.
Cut chuck roast into large chunks, salt and pepper the meat generously.
Chop an onion in half and peel the outer layer, also peel the head of garlic. Pull stems off of chilies and deseed them.
Then in a very hot instant pot, cover the bottom with a layer of oil (2 tbsp) and sear the meat on all sides (work in sections).
Once the meats been seared on both sides, remove onto a sheet pan.
Add in another tbsp of oil and place the onion flat till it’s fully seared/blackened, then remove.
Toss in the the whole garlic cloves, 1 tbsp pepper corns, a cinnamon stick, 3 cloves, and a bayleaf to bloom spices in the remaining oil.
Once they’re fragrant, add in a sliced tomato and also try charring it.
Add onions back in and pour water till just covered, bring to a boil till softened. Pour all of it into a blender and mix till smooth before pouring back into the instant pot.
Add meat and strain the blended salsa back into the pot, add in extra water till covered along with some apple cider vinegar and chicken bouillon.
Once dissolved: and add these spices to taste:
Cook in instant pot on high pressure for 45 minutes.
Wait till the pressure is fully released before opening and removing the meat and shred.
Lightly brush consommé on corn tortillas, fry before topping with about 11g of oaxaca cheese and the meat, fry till crispy and serve with consommé, lime and cilantro and fresh onion.
Marinate shrimp and set aside for maybe half an hour atleast
Into a mixer: Chopped onions, chillies, ginger garlic paste, and all the spices.
Pour 2 tbsp of oil into a pan, once heated fry shrimp till 50% cooked, then remove.
Pour in another tbsp of oil and pour in the onion paste and curry leaves. Fry till it comes together.
Add 1/3 cup of water + the 50% cooked shrimp. Again cook till the onion paste sticks to the shrimp making a thick curry.
Top w/ garam masala and coriander and serve with rice.
Generously salt chicken on both sides and let sit for 10 minutes.
In a saucepan, heat water till it comes to a boil.
Add in all of the chicken into the stock. Try to ensure they’re as submerged as possible.  Put a lid on and let braise on low for about 20 minutes.
Process Vegetables - Chop up on onion into large chunks, de-husk tomatillos and cut in half, destem and cut in half the pablanos and jalapano peppers.
Preheat the broiler to high. Then, peel the garlic cloves and spread the garlic along with the vegetables onto a sheet pan with some parchment paper.
Toss the tray under the broiler for 15 minutes till they’re nicely charred and caramelized.

Move everything from the sheetpan (except for one of the roasted poblanos) into a blender and blend on high for about 1 minute until smooth. Then dd in some fresh cilantro at this point and blend again.
Remove chicken and let rest till cooled slightly.
To the chicken cooking liquid, add the pureed tomatillo sauce along with some better than bouillon chicken base.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for about 20 minutes, reducing the sauce by 30-40%.
Shred the chicken into threads that are about 1/4 inch wide, should still be a little chunky and not over shredded to prevent it from becoming mushy.
Grate the smoked gouda.
Taste the enchilada sauce, taste and add salt to taste at this point.
Pour 100g of the tomatillo sauce as well as half of the grated cheese into the bowl with the shredded chicken, stir till well combined.

On a nonstick pan, heat the tortillas as you go so they’ll remain pliable, else they’ll crumble as we roll them! Also, prepare a sheet pan with parchment paper to the side and preheat the oven to 450*F.

Once tortillas are gently heated, brush tomatillo salsa to both sides of tortilla, spoon in about an 1/8 of the chicken cheese filling (about 65g) into the tortilla. Roll tightly and place onto the sheet pan tray with the seam side down. Make a second pair that sits snugly next to the first wrapped tortilla.
Continue till you have 4 pairs of neatly rolled tortillas on the sheet pan, with each pair touching and about an inch space between each pair.
Cover each pair with a few spoonful's of the tomatillo sauce, spread to just cover the tortillas without drowning them.
Top each pair with their quarter of the remaining shredded cheese (about 40g each).
Put into the oven for 10-12 minutes till the cheese is starting to brown.
While the enchiladas are baking, add in some sour cream, the last roasted poblano, and some water and mix till smooth to make poblano crema.

Once fully baked, to serve, optionally top with some shredded little gem or romaine lettuce for crunch. Add a generous spoonful of the prepared crema and top with fresh cilantro and the jalapeno slices from earlier.
Preheat oven to 300°F. Place rack in the lower section of the oven. Fit baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone mats.

To the bowl of a food processor (ideally fitted with a metal blade) add confectioners’ sugar, almond flour, and hazelnut flour. Process into a fine powder, about 30 seconds. Using a normal strainer, sift mixture onto a sheet of parchment paper three times and discard any coarse meal (no more than 1/2 teaspoon). Set aside.

Onto the Meringue → To a bowl, add egg whites and cream of tartar, use a whisk attachment to whip whites on medium speed until foamy. Gradually add sugar, stopping to scrape down the sides of the bowl as needed.
Once all the sugar is incorporated, continue to whip meringue into stiff glossy peaks. The meringue should have the consistency of shaving cream.

Now the macaronnage → add one-third of the sifted flour mixture to the meringue and fold with the spatula. Once incorporated, add the remaining flour mixture, smearing the batter along the sides of the bowl and then folding back to the center, being careful not to over mix. Repeat until the batter becomes shiny and reaches the consistency of slow-moving lava.

Transfer batter to a pastry bag fitted with a 1/2” plain round tip. Using both hands to hold the pastry bag, pipe batter by placing the tip 1/2” above the parchment, directly in the center of the first stenciled circle, and fill. Once the circle is filled, stop squeezing and make a quick upward and sideways motion to detach the batter and move to the next circle. (The batter should settle into a smooth and glossy round circle.)

Let macarons stand at room temperature until a firm skin forms on the macarons, 30 to 45 minutes. To check to see if the macarons are ready, lightly touch the side, and if your finger does not make a dent, they are ready for the oven.

Bake macarons one sheet at a time, rotating halfway through, until macarons are crisp and firm, 14 to 16 minutes. Carefully slide macaron topped parchment sheets onto a wire rack and allow macarons to cool completely before removing from paper.
For chocolate ganache:
For buttercream filling (my preference):
Once filled, macrons should be refrigerated in an air tight container overnight to set (or just eat them but its better overnight!)

French macarons are notoriously one of the hardest desserts to make, but there are only 5 ingredients! Here are some tips to make sure they turn out right every time!
1. Make sure your measurements are precise! If you pack your ingredients into measuring cups, your ratios may be off.
2. Ensure the meringue is whipped to stiff peaks. Over or under-mixing will negatively affect the texture.
3. When folding the macaron batter, the texture should fall off the spatula like a ribbon. Over-mixing will result in flat cookies and under-mixing makes the top of the cookies burst.
4. Allow the cookies to dry on the sheet for 30 minutes before baking to ensure the feet form!

2 cups of powdered sugar. 1 cup of almond flour. Sift - whisk together (set it aside). Separate 3 eggs and add just the 3 egg whites to the bowl mixture thing. Gradually add 1/4 cup of sugar and some food colouring. It should be that thick. Fold the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients. Fold it carefully till it looks like molten lava. Pipe the macarons on parchment paper. Bang tray on the table or bench to get out air bubbles. Bake at 300degrees for 15-20mins. The sign of a true macaron should have a ring around the edge called "feet". Fill the macarons with what ever you want. He made a dolce delece??(something) pastry cream.

Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Fold a large sheet of parchment paper in half, then cut out a vaguely heart shape that can fit the fish inside of it.
Chop herbs and any aromatics you’d like to use, slice garlic and zest a lemon.
Melt down some butter in a small pan before adding in the chili flakes and garlic till fragrant. Take off heat and toss in the herbs and lemon zest.
Fold parchment papers in half and place a bed of half of any aromatics you want to use at the bottom of each paper.
Pat fish pieces completely dry and place onto the bed of aromatics. Generously salt and pepper the fish before pouring on the melted butter and generously drizzle with fresh lemon juice all over.
Fold over and fully enclose the fish fillet into the sealed packet, as securely as possible.
Place the parchment packets on a baking sheet and bake for 10 minutes.
After 10 minutes, turn off the oven and let the salmon sit inside for an additional 5–10 minutes.
Carefully remove the packet from the oven, open them, and serve the salmon hot with rice and/or vegetables.
Combine all ingredients for the meat filling, mix well.
Cut pita bread in half and gently open up to make pita pockets.
Divide meat into fourths (approx 50g portions) and place into the pita bread pockets, pressing to spread till the edges, ensuring they’re spread evenly.
Lightly spray pan with olive oil and cook pita breads for two minutes on each side till golden and crispy!
Serve with a greek yogurt or toum dip!
Roast hazelnuts in oven at 350* for 10 - 12 minutes till toasty.
Toss into a kitchen towel while still warm and rub together till the skin mostly rubs off
Toss hazelnuts into a food processor (about 10 minutes) and blend till it turns into a shiny butter. (scrape down as you go)
Pour in rest of the ingredients (milk chocolate, sugar, cacao, salt, oil, vanilla extract)
In a blender (or mixing bowl), pour in all the ingredients and mix till well combined.
Rest the batter in the fridge overnight if possible.
Heat a flat skillet and brush with butter.
Pour in the batter.
Let sit for 30 - 45 seconds before flipping.
Once flipped, just let sit for 10 - 15 seconds or till very lightly browned.
Homemade Ladyfingers
Preheat oven to 350℉

Create a double boiler by adding a few inches of water to a medium saucepan that is wide enough to hold your stand mixer bowl without the base of the bowl going low enough to touch the water. Bring the water to a simmer.
While the water comes to a simmer, whisk together the eggs and sugar in the bowl of a stand mixer until just combined.

Place the stand mixer bowl over the simmering water, add a candy thermometer to the side of the bowl so it sits in the egg mixture and whisk constantly until the mixture reaches 160℉ (71℃), about 5 minutes.

Fit your stand mixer with the whisk attachment. Add the stand mixer bowl with the heated egg mixture in it and whip on high speed for 7-10 minutes or until the eggs have increased by 2.5 times their original volume and very soft peaks have formed; the mixture will overall have the texture of soft serve ice cream.
Whisk in the vanilla extract and then remove from the stand mixer.
Fold in the flour until it just combined; do not overmix or you will deflate the eggs.
Transfer the mixture to a piping bag fitted with a round tip.

Line two baking sheets with parchment paper and pipe the ladyfingers so that they are about 3” (7.5cm) long and 1” (2.5cm) wide. Make sure there is at least 1” (2.5cm) of space between each ladyfinger and continue piping until all the batter is used; you should have about 30 ladyfingers in the end.

It is very important to bake these one tray at a time. Right before baking, generously dust the tops of the ladyfingers with powdered sugar. Place in the oven and bake for 8-12 minutes or until lightly golden and crisp.
Remove the ladyfingers to a cooling rack to cool completely and repeat baking the remaining ladyfingers.
Espresso Soak:
Whisk together all the ingredients in a shallow baking dish until the sugar is dissolved and everything is evenly combined. Set aside.
‍Tiramisu Cream:

Set up another double boiler by adding a few inches of water to a medium saucepan that is wide enough to hold a medium bowl without the base of the bowl going low enough to touch the water. Bring the water to a simmer.

Add the egg yolks and sugar to the medium bowl and place over the double boiler. Whisk constantly until the sugar is almost completely dissolved. Switch to an electric hand blender and whip the mixture until it is 2.5 times its original volume, it has lightened in color, and it holds a very soft peak that falls back in ribbons when lifted. Remove the bowl from the heat and let cool slightly.
Add the mascarpone to a separate medium bowl and whip with the hand blender until very smooth. Fold the mascarpone into the egg mixture until evenly combined.

To the same bowl you whipped the mascarpone in, add the heavy cream and use the hand blender to whip until soft peaks form. Add the mascarpone and egg mixture to the whipped cream and fold until the whipped cream is just incorporated and you no longer see white streaks in the mixture. Be very gentle as you fold. Set aside.
‍Tiramisu Assembly:

Have a 9x9” (23x23cm) baking dish set up next to the espresso mixture and cooled lady fingers. Dip the ladyfingers, 1 or 2 at a time, into the espresso and let soak for just a few seconds per side. If the lady fingers sit in the espresso too long they will fall apart. Place one layer of ladyfingers in the baking dish, making sure there is no overlapping.
Add half of the tiramisu cream over the top, spreading it from edge to edge in the pan.
Add another layer of the soaked ladyfingers, followed by the remaining tiramisu cream.
Cover the baking dish with plastic wrap, making sure it doesn’t touch the top of the cream. Place in the fridge to set overnight or up to 2 days in advance.
Before serving, dust the top of the cream with cocoa powder. Cut the tiramisu into 9 even pieces and place on a plate. Garnish with finely grated dark chocolate and serve.
In a bowl, mix some ground meat with some salt, pepper, garlic powder, cayenne, msg, and Italian seasoning along with a tsp of oil. Once well combined, roll into three balls.
On a skillet, with a spray of oil, toast the tortillas. Once toasted on both sides, place ball of meat onto the pan, add a tortilla on top and smash down till fully flattened with a panini press.
Let cook and caramelize, then flip over, add cheese and fold the tortilla in half, putting the weight back on the taco.
Add any toppings + burger sauce and serve!
In the dutch oven, add in all the ingredients for the chicken marinade. Mix to combine and really massage everything together for at least 2 - 3 minutes to release all the flavors.
Then, add in the chicken, massage everything together again for another minute or two.
Add in ghee and optionally cream before mixing again.
Let marinate at least 3 to 4 hours, ideally, overnight!
Soak basmati rice in water for an hour.
In boiling water, add in all the spices. Bring to an aggressive boil and taste, should be very salty (10% salinity?).
While waiting for the water to boil, in a small pan, pour 30 ml of oil in a pan along with half an onion sliced roughly. Soften till soft and sweet and set aside.
At this point, also preheat the oven to 400*F.
Then, to the boiling water, add in the rice along with cilantro and mint, some lime juice, rose water and green chilllies that have been sliced down the middle.
Once the rice is 70% cooked, drain out the water (setting aside a specified amount).
Take half of the water that was set aside and pour it into the chicken that’s been marinating and mix the chicken again to add moisture back into the marinade.
Next, layer the drained rice on top of the chicken till fully covered.
On top, drizzle ghee, all of the excess oil that was used to caramelize the onions, the rest of the water that was used to boil the rice, and the saffron steeped in milk.
Finally top with caramelized onions, fried onions, cashews, cilantro, and small pinch of garam masala.
Toss into the oven at 400 for 20 minutes, then reduce to 350 for another 40 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 420F.
Lightly grease a 9-inch springform pan and layer it with parchment paper.
In a small bowl, whisk the corn starch and flour. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, add room temperature cream cheese, mascarpone, and sour cream. Mix with spatula until the mixture is smooth and creamy.
Add the heavy whipping cream and vanilla and mix again till combined well.
Sift in the flour mixture and gently fold it into the batter until combined.
In another bowl, using a hand mixer, beat the sugar and eggs until fluffy, for about 2-4 min.
Add the egg mixture into the cheesecake mixture and stir well.
Pour the cheesecake batter into the prepared pan.
Bake for 25 minutes and then decrease the oven to 400F. Continue to bake for 10 to 12 minutes.
Remove from the oven and cool. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 6-7 hours, or better overnight.
Serve plain, or with chocolate ganache!

Add ingredients except for butter to a mixer and spin until broken down into a sandy texture. Drizzle in butter while spinning. When ready, it should be able to hold itself together when you squeeze it. If it’s still crumbly, spin in a bit more butter.

Spread into a greased 9” springform pan by pressing 50-60% of the graham cracker mixture evenly against the sides using a glass. Then add remaining crumbs into the pan and press into an even layer on the bottom of the pan.
Par bake at 350F/175C for 10 minutes

Mix cream cheese, goat cheese, sugar, salt, and corn starch using paddle attachment on  medium speed, scraping sides of bowl after about 30 seconds. Mix for another minute or so until well combined. Add in sour cream, lemon zest, and vanilla and continue to mix for another minute until combined. Scrape paddle and bowl well. With mixer on medium, add eggs, 1 at a time, mixing until just combined, scraping sides halfway through. Careful not to over mix/whip after the eggs are added.
Pour mixture into fully cooled crust shell.

Bake at 325F/160C for 60 minutes. At this point, the cheesecake should have risen and still be jiggly in the center. Turn off oven and allow to cool in the oven, door closed for 45 minutes. Remove from the oven and allow to come to room temperature. Tent with foil and refrigerate for at least 10 hours.
Preheat your oven to 375 F.
Peel potatoes and cut them into big chunks and toss them into a large pot of cold water with baking soda and salt.
Bring the water to a boil and let cook till potatoes are fork tender, about 10 minutes. Drain the potatoes, and toss to roughen outside of the potato.
While the potatoes are boiling, put your sheet tray in the oven and preheat for 10 minutes.

In a small pan, heat oil with garlic cloves, chili flakes and rosemary till toasted and infused. Then drain out the infused oil into the potatoes. Add in some garlic powder and toss again till evenly combined.
Toss potatoes onto the preheated sheet tray and roast in the oven for 30 - 40 minutes, flipping and stirring every 15 minutes till golden and crispy.
Add back the toasted garlic, chili flakes and rosemary after taking out of the oven and toss again to combine.
Combine water, vinegar, sugar, & salt in pot, bring to boil till sugar dissolves.
Add sliced red onions into a heat proof container.
Remove water mixture from heat, pour over onions and let onions cool in liquid.
Refrigerate overnight, can stay for up to a month.
In a bowl, add chopped dark chocolate and milk chocolate. Heat cream and pour over the chocolate. Let sit till melted, then stir till you have a smooth ganache.
In a pot,  add the water, butter, sugar and salt. Place this onto medium heat and heat until all the butter has melted and the mixture comes to a boil. Then add in vanilla extract.
Add in flour, combine off heat before putting back on heat and combining for another 2 minutes.
Place this mixture into a large bowl and let cool for 10 minutes.
Add in egg and mix into a smooth dough, before adding into a large piping bag with a star shaped tip.
Heat oil, pipe dough into oil using scissors to cut it, and let sit till golden (flip if necessary) before moving them off to a wire rack or paper towel.
Mix together cinnamon and sugar, and while churros are still warm, coat them in sugar and set aside.
Serve churros with ganache!

For Garlic Confit: Preheat oven to 250*F. Combine all ingredients in a small oven safe ramekin, ensuring everything including the herbs are tucked into the oil. Then bake uncovered for 2 hours. Let cool completely before storing in an air tight container in the fridge for up to a week.
Add potatoes and 1 tbsp salt to a pot. Cover with cold water till covering potatoes by 1”. Boil for 15-20 minutes till potatoes are fork tender (can stick a fork through it easily)
While that’s boiling, melt your butter and add some sage to fry while the butter browns. Set sage aside for topping when done.

When the potatoes are done, drain very well and then add back into the pot over low heat to evaporating any remaining water. Start mashing the steaming potatoes till all the chunks are broken down. Add in Boursin cheese, garlic confit, the brown butter, and slowly pour in milk a little at a time, gently mashing as you go to get to desired consistency.
Add in about 2 oz of parmesan, another tbsp of garlic confit, and large dallop of sour cream, fold till incorporated. Top with extra brown butter and sage.

To make tangzhong: Mix flour and water together in a small pot till completely dissolved and no lumps remain. Then, place pot over medium heat and begin stirring till the mixture heats up and begins to thicken. When the mixture reaches 149* F, take the mixture off the stove and let it cool. Place mixture into a bowl, place plastic wrap on top, having it touching the top of the mixture to keep it from drying out. Let cool for a bit, maybe an hour.
Combine the flour, salt, sugar and instant yeast in a large bowl. Make a well in the center.
In a separate bowl, whisk together all wet ingredients: egg, milk and tangzhong. Add into the well of the dry ingredients.

Use the dough hook attachment and mix on low speed until your dough comes together. Add in the butter and continue mixing on medium to high speed. Mix/knead for about 18-20 minutes until the dough is no longer sticky and is elastic.

To test the elasticity, use the windowpane test. Take a piece of dough and stretch it out. You should be able to stretch it until the dough becomes a very thin membrane that is almost sheer and lets in light. In addition, when you poke a hole in the thin membrane, it should form a close to perfect circle. If the dough breaks before you can stretch the dough this far, it is not ready.
Gather the dough into a ball shape. Take a large bowl and grease with oil. Place dough into a greased bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Let it proof until it’s doubled in size, about 40 minutes.
Deflate and divide the dough into nine equal portions. Knead into ball shapes. Cover with plastic wrap and it let rest for 15 minutes.

Take one portion and roll with a rolling pin into an oval shape. Take one end of the dough and fold to meet the middle of the oval. Take the other end and fold to meet on top. Turn the dough over, so that the folds face down and roll and flatten dough with rolling pin. Flip dough again, so folded side faces up. Roll the dough up from top to bottom. Take both ends and fold down until they meet at the bottom. Stretch and move the top portion of the dough around until you end up with a ball shape at the top and the ends are tucked into the bottom. Repeat this step of rolling for the rest of your dough. (Reference video at the b

With seals of the dough balls facing down, place the nine balls into an 8 x 8 baking pan that is lined with parchment paper. Then cover with plastic wrap. Leave it for the second round of proofing, about 45 to 60 minutes, until double in size.
Brush egg wash on surface of buns (this will create the shiny finish). Bake in a pre-heated 330°F oven for 30-35 minutes, or until golden brown.

Melt  butter with sage leaves till crips, remove the sage leaves and let cool. Once cooled, crush and add back into the butter with honey. Remove from heat and brush over buns when they’re fresh out of the oven. Top with some flaky salt and transfer onto a wire rack and let cool completely.
Making the lamb:
Place a pot onto medium heat and add some oil into this.
Add the chopped onions and cook these until they turn brown.
Add the garlic paste, ginger paste, cumin seeds and onion seeds, give this a mix. Then add your lamb and lemon juice, mix all this together.
The lamb is going to release water, you just want to cook it on medium heat until the meat has changed color and most of the water has gone.
Now add the yoghurt, chopped green chillies, chopped tomatoes and all the seasonings EXCEPT the garam masala (we're going to add this at the end). Mix all this together.
Add some water into the pot, you want just enough water so that the lamb is covered.
Lower your heat to low, then add a lid and cook until all the water has gone. We are cooking this on low heat so it will take a while, but you will be left with soft lamb so the wait is worth it.
Once the water has gone, mix in the garam masala, and then your lamb is done.
The rice:
Just before the lamb is almost ready, cook your rice. Cook this however you like to cook rice.
Layering:
I started by cooking my chopped onion in a pan, with some oil, just until they were soft and brown.
Then for the saffron milk, I just crushed the saffron and mixed this with the warm milk. Let this infuse for a few minutes.
In a large pot, add a little bit of oil, then add half of the lamb. Add half of the cooked rice on top of this.
Drizzle half of the saffron milk over this rice, sprinkle some biryani masala over it, then add half of the cooked onions, some chopped jalapeños and then some coriander.
Add the rest of the lamb on top of this, then add the rest of the rice.
Now add some more saffron milk, biryani masala, onions, jalapeños and coriander. At this point I also added a few slices of lemon on top.
Cover this pot tightly with some tin foil, then add a lid.
Place this over LOW heat and let this cook for 30 minutes. Make sure to keep the heat low, otherwise the lamb at the bottom of the pot could burn.
After 30 minutes, take it off the heat and open the pot. Gently mix the layers together and then the biryani is done.
Heat half of the can of coconut milk in a deep pan for 2 - 3 minutes till bubbling and slightly thickened.
Then, add in curry paste and stir till fully incorporated. Then simmer and stir for 5 - 6 minutes till oil separates from the paste and the mixture becomes dark and toasted.
Add in the sliced chicken and the vegetables, and stir till fully coated in the curry.
Once toasted, add in the rest of the coconut milk and 3 - 4 torn kaffir lime leaves.
Cover and simmer on medium low for 10 - 15 minutes, stirring occasionally to ensure the bottom doesn’t burn, until the chicken is cooked through.
Season with palm sugar and fish sauce, toss in basil leaves and stir till wilted.
Garnish with sliced red chilies and serve over rice!
Start by pre-heating your oven to 180c/350f. Also grease and line a 10 inch square cake tin, leave this aside.
To Make the cookie dough: Melt your butter, then pour this into a large bowl along with both sugars and the vanilla. Aggressively whisk this for 1 minute.
Add the egg and whisk this in.
In a separate bowl, mix together the flour, baking powder and salt.
Add the flour mixture into your other bowl, along with the chopped chocolate. Mix everything together just until you can no longer see any flour.
Leave this cookie dough in your fridge whiles you make the brownie batter.
Making the brownie batter: In a pot, add the butter and dark chocolate. Place this onto medium heat and heat until melted, you can also just do this in your microwave. Leave this aside to cool down.
In a large bowl, add the eggs and both sugars. Whip these together until your mixture lightens in colour and increases in volume slightly. This should take about 2 minutes.
Pour the melted butter and chocolate mixture into this, whisk this in.
Now add the flour, cocoa powder and salt, whisk just until combined.

Baking the brookie: Get your prepared tin. Grab pieces of the cookie dough and place these into your tin, don't cover the base of the tin completely with the cookie dough, leave gaps between the pieces.

Pour your brownie batter on top of this and spread it out. Now add more pieces of cookie dough on top of the brownie batter, you don't want to cover the brownie batter completely, you want to still be able to see it.
You will have some cookie dough left, we're going to use this later.
Place this into your pre-heated oven and bake for 30 minutes.
After 30 minutes, take it out of the oven. Press pieces of the left over cookie dough into the top of the brownie.
Place this back into your oven and bake for about another 20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the centre comes out with a few moist crumbs, from the brownie, on it.
Once baked, optionally sprinkle some flaky salt on top.
Leave to cool at room temperature for 20 minutes, then place it into your fridge and leave to cool and set up for 1 hour.
After an hour, cut into it and now your brookies are done. Enjoy!
Start by making jam, can store in fridge well!

If topping with prosciutto: lay the prosciutto slices on a parchment paper lined baking sheet at bake for 12-15 minutes, until darkened in color. watch them to avoid burning as different pieces crisp up differently. Let cool fully on the baking sheet then using the back of your spoon or hands, crumble into small pieces.
Preheat oven to 400 F.
Working quickly, place puff pastry sheet onto a parchment lined bakings sheet.
Stamp out 15-16 circles, try getting as close as you can to the borders to optimize the amount of puffs per sheet. Reroll and repeat.
Now, for the inner circle, with a little bit of pressure, indent the center with your smaller cookie cutter, but do not go all the way through. prick the center with a fork 2-3 times.
Place in the fridge while you work on the second sheet. Both sheets should be in the fridge for 10-15 minutes to encourage a good puff.
Remove from the fridge and brush the outer edges (not the inner circle) with egg wash, being careful not to “glue” the inner indentation you made shut.
Sprinkle the entire dough round with the crushed peppercorns + thyme leaves + flaky salt and bake until puffed up, and golden brown, about 8-10 minutes.
Cut brie into bite sized pieces.
Remove puffs from oven, and using a small spoon, gently tap down the centers of the puff - they should easily collapse.

Fill with a heaping 1/2 tsp of jam and snuggle in a cube of brie along side. pop them back in the oven for another 5-8 minutes until the brie is all melted and the pastry is fully golden and gorgeous.
Add toppings on top, then enjoy warm or even room temp!
Preheat oven to 425*F
Fill a large pot with salt, all the potatoes, and just enough water to cover the potatoes by an inch. Bring water to a boil then reduce to a simmer and let simmer till fork tender (about 15 minutes)
Transfer onto a sheet tray lined with parchment paper or aluminum foil and let steam till cool enough to handle.
Melt butter, garlic, olive oil, oregano, basil, and any herbs till fragrant.
Spray sheet with some oil, then smash potatoes down into flat patties (more you smash the crispier they’ll become)
Season with salt, pepper, and cayenne, drizzle with olive oil and bake for 20 minutes.
After 20 minutes, pull potatoes out and spoon the herb garlic butter over each potato before putting back in the over for another 10/15 minutes.
Serve with extra herbs and sea salt!
Mix together marinade ingredients to taste before adding in protein.
When cooking,  skewer them and pan fry, or bake (375F for 15-18 mins, 2-3 min broil at the end)
Soak all base ingredients in a container in the fridge overnight
Add 2 tsp of vanilla extract
Blend on high for 2 minutes
Strain, blend, strain, blend
Stir in 1 can each of evaporated and condensed milk
Heat water in a kettle to 176*
In a bowl, measure out 3 tsp of sifted matcha powder, pour over about a quarter cup of water till covered, whisk till smooth and foamy.
Into a jar, measure out 7 oz of milk.
Add brown sugar syrup to the milk and froth to combine before adding in matcha and combining again.
Optionally top with Cold Foam
Onto a sheet pan, spread all the bones evenly onto the pan so they’re not touching, spray them gently with oil and put into an oven that’s 425* for 30 - 40 minutes till bones are fully browned.
Into a stock pot, add in bones and fill up enough cold water to cover all the bones. (discard the oil from the bones, you don’t want it in your broth)
Let sit in the cold water for 5 minutes. Then place on stove and bring to a simmer on medium heat for 30 minutes - don’t bring to a boil at this step yet (removing any scum as it forms)
Char veggies and place them face up onto a sheet tray and broil till blackened (10 - 12 minutes, keep an eye on them)
Add charred veggies into the broth + the fish sauce, bouillon, and sugar.
Simmer for 30 minutes.
Toast spices in a small pan till slightly fragrant (be careful not to burn!!)
Add spices into the soup.
Simmer for another 30 minutes to a few hours.
Pour broth through a colander, discard all of the excess spices and bones.
Optionally strain through a fine mesh strainer to get a smooth base.
Taste and season with fish sauce for salt and sugar to balance - to taste here.
Add Cilantro Stems to a cheese cloth, tie securely and allow to steep in the broth as it cools.
Cook rice noodles by adding rice noodles to boiling water for about 10 - 15 seconds before adding to a bowl.
Then top noodles with chicken, green onions, cilantro, sliced onions, thai basil, mint, etc.
Top with the broth and serve with sliced jalapenos and a slice of lime! Add hoisin and sambal for extra flavor and spice.
Start by making miso glaze:
Fully dry fish fillets (very important, really dry them)
Throw fish filets into a container, and really massage with the miso glaze, then set into the fridge for as long as possibly (ideally overnight, up to 1 - 2 days)
Place fish onto a sheet tray, brush on a little bit more of the miso glaze.
Throw into broiler till nicely charred (6-8 minutes)
Steep the tea leaves in hot off-the-boil water for 5 minutes and then strain through a fine mesh strainer.
Add the sugar and salt and stir to dissolve. Allow to cool to room temp before making tea so it won't dissolve the ice too much. This is the thai tea base!
Pour thai tea base into glass and top with cream!
Start by pre-heating your oven to 400f. Also line an 8 inch cake tin with a large piece of baking paper.
In a large bowl, add the cream cheese, sugar, corn starch and salt. Beat these together until smooth.
Add half of the double cream into this, mix until smooth then mix in the rest of the cream.
Mix in the yoghurt, lemon juice and vanilla extract. Then finally, add in the eggs and mix just until you get a smooth batter.
Pour this batter into your tin, smooth it out, and then slam the tin down on your counter a few times to get rid of any bubbles.

Bake this around 40-45 minutes. It is done when the edges are set, but the centre is still quite jiggly. When you get to this point, if the cheesecake still isn't burnt on top, don't bake it for longer. Just put it under your grill for a few minutes.
Once baked, leave to cool at room temperature for an hour, and then leave it in the fridge for at least two hours, or overnight, to set.
Once set, cut into it, serve with some melted chocolate and enjoy!
Pour thai tea into the hot water and let the tea steep for 8 minutes.
Strain a few times to get any small tea leaves out.
Pour the strained tea into the pot, then add evaporated, condensed milk, and sugar.
Mix till distributed before adding the gelatin packets and bringing to a simmer over a low to medium heat.
Add in the rum, mix, then pour into molds and allow them to set for 4-5 hours.
Shake till well incorporated, serve over ice!
Peel the pears and remove the bottom where the fibers are. Make sure to keep the stalks on!
Add the pears to a large pot, big enough for all the pears to sit in one layer without sticking out. Add the granulated sugar, the red wine, the lemon peel and the cinnamon stick.
Cover the pot and simmer over low heat until the pears are tender. To check, gently insert a toothpick or the tip of a knife — if it goes in without resistance, they’re ready.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool completely. Strain the poaching liquid and discard the flavorings.
Add the pears back into the poaching liquid and keeping them in the fridge overnight to eat them the next day or within three days.
Before Eating:
Plate each pear with a dallop of mascarpone cream and sauce poured on top.
Add both to a mason jar and infuse for a week.
In a large bowl, make your base by adding together your water and gelatin and let sit.
In a saucepan, make your syrup by adding in your sugar, corn syrup and water slowly and bring over medium heat, make sure everything is melted before bringing it to a boil
Once the syrup is boiling, use a candy thermometer to check its at 240f.
Once it reaches 240f, take it off the heat, slowly pour it into your gelatin base and start whisking on high for ~8 minutes
Check to see the consistency to make sure it’s thick, shiny and can still hold it’s shape (it should not be fully stiff peaks as this will make the mixture hard to work with and shape)
Coat your baking pan with a thin coat of oil and a dusting of powdered sugar and corn starch
Pour into your baking pan and smooth out the best that you can
Dust again with a coat of powdered sugar + corn starch and let it set for ~4 hours or overnight
Using a knife coated with a thin layer of oil and dusted with the powdered sugar mixture, cut your marshmallow into cubes
Chop up all vegetables and set aside.
Cut up chicken breast into bite sized (1 inch) pieces.
In a large pot, add 12 cups of water and the chicken breast pieces. Bring water to a boil before removing from heat and leave off-heat, covered for 15 minutes.
Remove chicken from the water into a separate container.
Throw in chicken bouillon paste and orzo into the pot, bring to a light boil and let cook for 15 minutes covered.

In a separate pan, sauté all the chopped vegetables with a spray of oil and sprinkle of salt. Once roasted, toss into a container with a little cold water, then blend with an immersion blender till smooth.

Then add a little bit of the boiled orzo, then blend with an immersion blender or mixer. Then add in egg yolks and some hot water,  blend once again. Toss into the large pot along with the cooked chicken and more water as needed.
Also add in chopped carrots and peas, and bring to a boil and/or high heat.
Whip up egg whites till stiff peaks.
Zest lemons and juice into a container. Add lemon zest into the pot.
Mix a little bit of water with cornstarch and flour till not clumpy, toss into the pot, stir for about a minute before removing from heat.
Mix in the lemon juice and whipped whites, as well as all spices. Add salt to taste!
Top with chives or hot sauce 😋
Choose a marinade for the fish, rub into fish well and let rest for as long as possible (30 minutes it’s fine)
Make spicy mayo by combining all ingredients.
Turn broiler on to high heat and let warm for about 10 minutes.
Toss fish right underneath the broiler for about 6 - 7 minutes till nice and charred. Then remove and let rest for just a minute.
Warm tortillas while fish cooks.
Build taco by roughly breaking fish into chunks, topping with some spicy mayo, and the toppings and serve!
Combine all meatball ingredients. Mix till just combined to keep tender. Form into small, 1/2 inch meatballs. Set aside.
In a large deep pot, char the meatballs on both sides. Don’t have to fully cook but get a nice sear. Once cooked, remove to a seperate bowl.
In the same pot, sauté onions, carrots, celery, and half the garlic till fragrant and soft.
Then add in uncooked orzo and cook till lightly toasted.
Deglaze with soju, then add in chicken stock. Toss in bay leaf, parmesan rind, and any other seasoning. Let simmer for 5 minutes.
Toss in the meatballs and the finely chopped spinach. Allow to finish simmering (another 5-10 minutes).
Toss in rest of the garlic and stir in right at the end. Taste for salt, pepper, add in a little more soju for brightness or add some vinegar or lemon juice!
Top with parmesan cheese and serve!
Make the filling by combining all the filling ingredients together and mixing well.
Add a little bit of the broth and combine till filling looks juicy rather than dry.
Spoon the filling till it fills bout a 1/3 of the ramekin, gently press flat
Place one dumpling wrapper directly on top of the filling
Spoon a little extra chicken  broth (about 1/2 tsp) over the wrapper. This keeps it soft and helps everything steam up extra juicy.
Place the bowls in a pot or onto a steamer and add atleast an inch of water, then cover and steam over medium heat for 15–20 minutes, until fully cooked and juicy.
Serve topped with chili oil, soy sauce, scallions, and sesame seeds
Mix ground meat with all the seasonings till well combined. Then add to a pan with a two sprays of oil, let fully cook and mash into chunky crumbles.
With another two sprays of oil and a hot pan, fry the egg. Top with a little salt and pepper and cook till the whites are set (might have to add a lid and a little water to let steam).
With the rest of the oil, coat the tortilla really well. Toast on both sides.
Then, leaving tortilla on the pan, top with the cooked meat, an egg, and then the cheese. Let sit on the stove on low till very crispy.
Once crispy to your liking, gently slide onto a plate, top with pickled onions and cilantro. Serve with some hot sauce!
Pat chicken completely dry before seasoning with salt, pepper, paprika, and  flour, and any other seasonings.
Place skin side down in a cold Dutch oven with a spray of oil. Bring up to medium heat. Let cook till golden on both sides, then set aside.
Reduce heat and add in onions with a sprinkle of salt, cook till soft.  (Add bell pepper here if using)
Then add tomato paste and cook for another minute or two, then garlic for another minute, then the paprika and cayenne.
Lightly toast all the spices before deglazing with some coconut milk and the chicken stock. Be sure to scrape off the caramelized bits at the bottom before bringing sauce to a boil.
Check seasoning for salt and spice before adding in the chicken, covering with a lid and letting it simmer gently for 30 minutes.
Temper some sour cream with the sauce before adding in to prevent curdling. Check seasonings and serve with a sprinkle of parsley! Best served over some egg noodles!

Combine all sauces into a bowl and combine.
Mince salmon till fine before adding to the bowl of sauce, mix and then store in fridge till ready.
Combine the ingredients for sushi rice before layering onto a plastic wrap, flattening it out and freezing for 2 hours to overnight!
Once fully frozen, slice rice into rectangles for frying.
Shallow fry till golden, then top with salmon mixture, a slice of jalapeño and sesame seeds.
Bring a bowl of salted water to a boil, cook pasta till al dente.
Prep ingredients, chop parsley, mince garlic, halve tomatoes, and cut brie.
Heat oil in a pan and cook cherry tomatoes and garlic in the pan till the tomatoes soften, about 5 minutes.
Add in the brie and allow to melt into a creamy sauce, stirring gently.
Add in the cooked pasta, reserved pasta water, salt and any other spices.
Toss till a little thickened and serve!
Combine tuna filling in a bowl and mix til evenly combined
Spread the butter slice with the room temperature butter till well coated
Cook one side of the bread, before flipping.
Then spread half the cheese, top with the tuna mixture, before adding the rest of the cheese.
Cover if needed, cook till cheese is fully melted and bread is golden.
Set nuts and milk aside to soak (for at least 30 minutes).
Add ghee into a pressure cooker with all the whole spices and ginger garlic paste till toasted and fragrant.
Add in the sliced onions and sauté till softened.
Then toss in green chilies and all of the powdered spices and toast for a 30 seconds.
Add in lamb and sear meat till no longer pink on the outside.
Add 3 cups of water and pressure cook. Let it whistle 5 - 6 times and let the steam release on it’s own!
Add 1/2 cups plain yoghurt and the nut paste and another 3 cups water and let it come to a boil.
Add saffron, extra salt and black pepper to taste. Top with chopped mint and chopped cilantro.
Simmer for another 30 minutes before serving.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a large bowl, combine the lamb mince, onions, spices, salt, one egg and peas. Mix well until evenly combined.
Crack the egg into a small bowl and beat lightly. Set aside.
Unroll the puff pastry. Spoon the keema mixture along one long edge of the pastry near the center, forming a neat sausage shape.
Brush the exposed edge with egg wash, fold over the filling and roll tightly to seal, use a fork and press down along the edge to seal on the closed edge.
Brush the top of the roll with egg wash and sprinkle with a combination of cumin, black mustard, and sesame seeds.
Cut the roll into even pieces and place upright on a lined baking tray, spacing them slightly apart.
Bake for 25–30 minutes, or until golden brown, crisp and cooked through. Serve hot or at room temperature.
Put the oil, butter and chopped onion in the pot and turn the heat on to medium.
Cook and stir the onion until it has become translucent, then add the chopped celery and carrot. Cook for about 2 minutes, stirring vegetables to coat them well.
Add ground beef, a large pinch of salt and a few grindings of pepper. Stir well and cook until the beef has lost its raw, red color, crumbling as it cooks.
Add milk and let it simmer gently, stirring frequently, until it has bubbled away completely. Add a tiny grating — about ⅛ teaspoon — of nutmeg, and stir.
Then add the wine, let it simmer until it has evaporated.

Then add the tomatoes and stir thoroughly to coat all ingredients well. When the tomatoes begin to bubble, turn the heat down so that the sauce cooks at the laziest of simmers, with just an intermittent bubble breaking through to the surface. Cook, uncovered, for 3 hours or more, stirring from time to time.
Toss with cooked drained pasta, adding the tablespoon of butter, and serve with freshly grated Parmigiano-Reggiano on the side.
To make Crust:
Make fillings
Make custard

Boil eggs for 7 minutes, then place into an ice bath. Peel immediately or they’ll be harder.
Mix together all marinade ingredients, then pour over eggs and fully cover.
Place in fridge for at least a day, up to a week.
Serve over a bed of rice with the marinade.
Whisk yogurt, salt, and sliced green chilies, and water till smooth with sliced chilies and salt.
In a small pan, heat oil and add in all of the spices till toasted. Then add in curry leaves and onion, cook till softened.
Mix in the yogurt mixture till combined before removing. Taste for salt and serve topped with cilantro.
In a bowl, cover the mushrooms with boiling water and let soak for 10 minutes. Drain, reserving 5 tablespoons of the soaking liquid.
Then, very finely chop the mushrooms, aiming to mimic the consistency of ground meat, then set aside.
Heat a medium pot of salted water to a boil.
Put the oil, garlic, crushed red pepper, parsley and ¼ teaspoon salt into a cold, large sauté pan over medium-low heat.
Very gently fry for 5 minutes until soft and lightly golden, turning the heat down if the garlic starts to brown.
Increase the heat to medium-high, then add the chopped mushrooms, tomato paste and plenty of freshly ground black pepper (about 50 twists). Stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the pasta in the salted boiling water according to package directions until al dente. Drain, reserving 1¾ cups of the pasta water.
Stir in 1½ cups of the reserved pasta water plus the reserved porcini soaking liquid into the pan with the mushrooms and bring to a simmer. Once simmering, let it bubble away for 3 minutes.

Add half the Parmesan, stirring until it has melted before adding the rest. Lower the heat to medium, then stir in the cream, followed by the drained tagliatelle. Toss over the heat until the pasta and sauce have emulsified (just 1 to 2 minutes) adding a splash more pasta water if the pasta looks dry.
Remove from the heat and serve at once with a sprinkling of parsley.
In a medium pot, add chicken, cold water (just enough to cover the chicken), ginger, garlic, scallions, shaoxing wine, and salt.

Bring water to a boil then turn down the heat to low until water is simmering. Cook chicken for 8-10 mins, half covered with a lid, until just cooked through (if you stick a chopstick or knife, juices should run clear). Save 1-2 tablespoon of poaching liquid for later.

Once the chicken is cooked through, immediately transfer chicken drumsticks to a large bowl of ice water to stop the cooking process. Once cooled down, shred the chicken with your hands and transfer chicken to a plate.
Toast the uncooked rice in a pan with some oil. Then add rice to the rice cooker and cook in the poaching liquid.
In a heat-proof bowl, add chili flakes, green onion, sesame seeds, grated garlic, and sugar.
In a small pot, heat oil until hot but not smoking (about 350F).
Pour hot oil in the bowl to sizzle the aromatics. Then add soy sauce, oyster sauce, chicken poaching liquid, and salt and mix well to incorporate. Set aside.
Pour the soy chili oil sauce over the chicken and mix well. Garnish with chopped scallions. Serve the chicken over white rice.
Using the sauté mode on the instant pot, add the ghee and sear the lamb in batches till deeply browned (3 - 4 minutes per side). Set aside when done.
In the same pot, sauté the onion till golden. Then add in ginger garlic paste, cook till raw smell disappears. Then add in tomato paste till it darkens and smells nutty.
Add all the spices and stir for 30 seconds till fragrant (don’t let them burn)
Deglaze with the stock, add in vinegar and return the lamb to the pot.
Pressure cook on high for 45 minutes, natural release for another 15.
Open the pot and measure the liquid, should have about 1 cup of liquid in the pot with the lamb removed. If there’s more, remove all extra liquid.
Add the washed and soaked rice and the saffron, then pressure cook for another 9 minutes.
Quick release and immediately fluff rice gently.
Optionally: Place lamb pieces onto a baking sheet and broil for 5 - 8 minutes till caramelized and charred.
Serve rice topped with broiled lamb and cilantro leaves.
Mix together marinade ingredients to taste before adding in protein.
When cooking,  skewer them and pan fry, or bake (375F for 15-18 mins, 2-3 min broil at the end)
Mix together marinade ingredients to taste before adding in protein.
When cooking,  skewer them and pan fry, or bake (375F for 15-18 mins, 2-3 min broil at the end)
Heat oil and fry the curry paste until it deepens in color.
Pour in coconut milk and water. Season with chicken bouillon, dark soy, soy sauce, and brown sugar. Bring to a boil.
Drop in the chicken leg quarters and drop heat to a simmer, let them be for 40-50 minutes until the meat is tender and cooked through.
Finish with fish sauce and lime juice for that perfect salty-bright balance.
Boil your noodles. Then fry a small handful of them until crispy in oil heated to ~350F.
To assemble, place unfried noodles into the bowl, then ladle that rich broth on top with a whole chicken piece.
Top with crispy noodles, cilantro, green onion, and a squeeze of lime.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Have the baking pan on hand because the caramel in the next step hardens quickly.
In a saucepan, combine granulated sugar and water. Heat on medium-low and do not stir. If you need to mix it, you can gently swirl the pan.

As it cooks, the sugar should dissolve, bubble, then turn from a cloudy white liquid to a light golden color. Once the caramel reaches an amber color, pour into your baking pan, evenly covering the bottom, tilting to distribute. Let caramel cool as you make the flan mixture.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together egg yolks and sweetened condensed milk until smooth.
Stir in evaporated milk and vanilla until combined. Use silicone spatula to scrape bottom and sides of bowl.
Pour flan mixture through sieve right into your baking pan.
Place baking pan into another baking tray with room temp water at the bottom so that the baking pan is sitting in 2-3 inches of water.
Lightly cover with oven-safe lid or aluminum foil.
Bake for 40-60 minutes, checking it periodically starting at the 30 minute mark.
Let cool (uncovered) at room temperature for 30-45 minutes. Lightly cover and chill in the fridge for a minimum of 3 hours (up to overnight) to allow it to fully set.

To serve, use a spatula to loosen the sides of the flan (run spatula along the sides 2 - 3 times). Then place your serving dish upside down on top of the baking dish. Flip over, tap on the baking dish, and the flan should slide out!
Preheat oven to 400* F
Slice baguette into half inch slices and spread onto a large baking sheet.
Top each piece with a thin slice of brie and a little piece of prosciutto.
Bake for 10 minutes
Slice apple slices very thin.
Top baguette pieces with apple slices and then drizzle with hot honey.
Char corn in the broiler or with a blow torch.
Cook shrimp on the pan for a minute with all the spices with a tsp of oil.
Dice all ingredients to the same size and combine.
Whisk dressing together, pour over the salad, and massage well. Top with cotija cheese and mix well again.
Taste for salt, acid, and spice and adjust!
Dice your onion, finely mince your garlic, and slice your sausages into bite-sized pieces.
Boil your noodles or tteokbokki for one minute before al dente, drain, and cool.
Oil up your pan and cook the onions and garlic for 2 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Add in your sausages and cook for one minute. Then stir in gochujang, gochutgaru, soy sauce, sugar, heavy cream, and milk.  Let simmer and reduce slightly.
Add your noodles or tteokbokki along with the mozerella cheese and stir. Continue simmering until the sauce fully coats and sticks to the noodles.
Serve in a bowl topped with green onions and a boiled egg on the side.
Into a blender, blend onions and green chilies into a paste (try to not use water)
Squeeze excess water out and place into a bowl with the 200g of meat. Add all the spices and knead for atleast one minute to make elastic and juicy.
Let meat rest for 30 minutes to overnight!
Place meat onto a large parchment paper. Place another parchment paper on top and roll very thin.
Remove the top parchment paper and then roll the lower parchment (about 1 inch pieces)
Bake at 425*F for 25 minutes.
Unroll and tear into pieces and broil for a minute to get crispy.
Serve with  rice and drizzle with white sauce.
Let pie sit at room temperature until the filling is soft and scoopable. Preheat oven to 350°F.

Scoop the entire pie including crust into a baking dish. Use a spatula to smash and mix everything together until chunky and combined. Stir in bourbon or apple cider if using, and spread evenly in the dish.
Make the streusel by combining flour, oats, brown sugar, cinnamon, cardamom, salt and pecans in a bowl.
Add the cold cubed butter and work it in with your fingertips until the mixture looks like rough crumbles with pea-sized butter pieces.
Scatter the streusel generously and evenly over the pie mixture. Cover tightly with foil and bake at 350°F for 30 minutes.
Then, remove foil and bake another 10-12 minutes until the streusel is golden, toasty and the edges are bubbling.
Serve by scooping into bowls while warm and top with a scoop of vanilla ice cream!
Boil ramen noodles to packet instructions.
In a bowl, add a egg yolk, the seasoning packets, kewpie mayo, sesame oil, soy sauce, and chicken bouillon. Whisk till combined.
Add water from boiling ramen into the sauce and mix.
Make a fried egg with the extra egg white and another egg.
Add noodles into the soup, top with fried egg.
Chop guanciale into equal strips and add it to a cold pan to cook over medium heat. Once crispy, remove it from the pan with its oil.
In the same pan, drizzle olive oil and add finely chopped shallots, garlic and basil leaves with the stem attached.
Squeeze in tomato paste and add gochujang to cook for 5 minutes.
While the tomato mix is cooking, add the pasta into salted boiling water. You want it 95% to aldente.
Deglaze tomato mix with vodka.
Remove the basil and cook some cream before adding cold butter. Add salt if needed.
Add the pasta with a splash of pasta water into the sauce and give everything a mix (add more pasta water if needed).
On a baking pan, layer the pasta and top with mozzarella, guancialle and parmesan before broiling on high heat for a minute till crispy.
9. Finish with fresh basil.
Combine all ingredients for the marinade in a resealable bag. Add the chicken and marinate for 30 minutes to overnight in the fridge.
Preheat air fryer to 400* F. Then, in a wide shallow bowl, stir together potato starch and sea salt.
Transfer marinated chicken into the potato starch and dredge till evenly coated.
Spray the pan with cooking spray, place the coated chicken in one layer, then spray the top again with cooking spray.
Air fry for 14 - 16 minutes, flipping half way through and spraying again, till crispy and crunch.
Serve immediately with kewpie mayo.
To a saucepan add espresso and gelatin packets and let sit and bloom for 1-2 minutes. Do not mix or heat yet.
Warm the saucepan over medium heat and stir for 2-3 minutes until all of the gelatin has dissolved.
Remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes.
Add the vodka and simple syrup to the saucepan with the espresso and mix.
Then divide the mixture in two bowls evenly.
Add the coffee liquor to one bowl and the baileys + room temp heavy cream to the other.
Spray an 8" x 8" pan with a neutral oil to help prevent sticking.
Pour the darker mixture in the pan and refrigerate for about 20 minutes. You want the first layer to be set but still tacky so the layers adhere to each other.
Then pour the baileys mixture on top and return to the refrigerator for at least 4 hours (overnight is best) or until completely set.
Flip pan upside down to remove and slice into squares as big or small as you would like.
Garnish each square with a coffee bean and enjoy!
Add elderflower liqueur and lemon juice, grapefruit juice, then add in mint leaves and muddle gently.
Fill glass full with ice.
Fill to the top with prosecco, then tonic water and club soda.
Stir well, top with more ice and garnish with grapefruit and mint.
Add all ingredients into a shaker bottle with lots of ice and shake well.
Strain into a glass over ice and garnish with a lime wheel and cherry.
